sections.heading: Everything you need to know before *reaching out*
sections.subheading: Common questions about how we work, what we build, and what to expect.

items.question: What kinds of projects does Fabrika take on?
items.answer: We specialize in AI-powered software: agents, automation systems, enterprise applications, and rapid MVP development. If your project involves AI, machine learning, or needs to move fast with high quality, we're a strong fit.

items.question: Do I need a technical background to work with you?
items.answer: No. Many of our best clients are non-technical founders or business operators. We handle all the technical decisions and keep you informed with plain-language updates and weekly demos.

items.question: How long does it take to build a product?
items.answer: Timelines depend on scope. An MVP or first major feature can be in your hands in 2–4 weeks for focused projects. A full-scale enterprise product typically takes 8–16 weeks from kickoff to launch.

items.question: How do you handle pricing?
items.answer: We scope projects after a discovery call so we can give you an accurate, fair estimate. Pricing varies by complexity. Most engagements range from project-based to ongoing retainer arrangements.

items.question: Who owns the code?
items.answer: You do. Everything we build is yours. We transfer full ownership of the codebase, documentation, and any IP created during the engagement.

items.question: Can you work with our existing tech stack?
items.answer: Yes. We're technology-agnostic and have experience across most major stacks. We'll work with what you have or recommend the best options for your goals.

items.question: Do you offer post-launch support?
items.answer: Yes. We offer ongoing retainer arrangements for teams that want continued development, maintenance, and iteration support after the initial launch.

items.question: What makes Fabrika different from a typical dev agency?
items.answer: We're an AI-first, senior team. We don't staff projects with junior devs or hand off to a PM layer. You work directly with engineers who understand both the business problem and the technical solution.
